examples: The first clouds are thin, wispy cirrus, followed by sheets of cirrus and cirrostratus and altostratus.
They are referred to as "alto" clouds and bear such names as altostratus, which are flat, or altocumulus (pictured), which are puffy.
Patchy, highly textured cumulus clouds are present at lower altitudes, while grey altostratus clouds are stretched out by prevailing winds at higher altitudes.
Altostratus is higher than stratus but beware.
